records.display_name: QBL-W First Interoperable CBTC for NYCT

payload.claim_text: QBL-W is the first interoperable CBTC project implemented for NYCT, allowing trains with CBTC to run on the same line at the same time with carborne and wayside CBTC equipment from different suppliers (Siemens and Thales).
payload.subject: Queens Boulevard Line West CBTC

records.raw_text: QBL-W is the first interoperable CBTC project implemented for NYCT, allowing trains with CBTC to run on the same line at the same time with carborne and wayside CBTC equipment from different suppliers (Siemens and Thales).

payload.claim_text: In 2021 the B&T BU exceeded its commitment plan by 50% while meeting the planned completion goal.
payload.subject: MTA Bridges and Tunnels Business Unit

records.raw_text: In 2021 the B&T BU exceeded its commitment plan by 50% while meeting the planned completion goal

payload.claim_text: A new street-to-concourse elevator in the 1 Times Square Building will be open to the public in May.
payload.subject: Grand Central - Times Square Shuttle ADA project

records.raw_text: a new street-to-concourse elevator in the 1 Times Square Building will be open to the public in May.

records.display_name: 8th Ave CBTC First to Use Axle Counters

payload.claim_text: The 8th Avenue CBTC project introduced Axle Counters in lieu of Insulated Joints/Track Circuits for the first time to reduce life-cycle costs and maintenance.
payload.subject: 8th Avenue Line CBTC

records.raw_text: This project builds on the technology introduced in previous CBTC projects, notably the CBTC interoperability achieved on the QBL-W line, and (for the first time) introduced Axle Counters in lieu of Insulated Joints/Track Circuits to reduce life-cycle costs and maintenance.

records.display_name: B&T Facilities in Sound Condition per FHWA

payload.claim_text: In accordance with FHWA ratings, MTA Bridges and Tunnels facilities are in sound condition, benefiting MTA's bond rating, lowering debt service costs and helping the MTA's overall financial position.
payload.subject: MTA Bridges and Tunnels

records.raw_text: in accordance with FHWA ratings, these critical revenue-producing assets are in sound condition, which directly benefits the MTA's bond rating, thus lowering debt service costs and helping the MTA's overall financial position.

payload.claim_text: B&T partners with regional transportation agencies to improve traffic flow, accessibility, and safety in the region whenever possible.
payload.subject: MTA Bridges and Tunnels

records.raw_text: B&T partners with regional transportation agencies to improve traffic flow, accessibility, and safety in the region whenever possible.

MTA C&D organization was created in 2019 to streamline, oversee, and improve regional capital planning, capital program development, the delivery functions of transit network expansion projects, and upgrades to stations and system-wide infrastructure.
MTA's construction and development arm responsible for capital program delivery
MTA agency responsible for construction and development projects. Referenced in BudgetWatch results for operating expenses and overtime.
MTA agency listed in Total Positions by Function and Agency table
MTA Construction & Development responsible for capital program delivery across MTA agencies including B&T, Infrastructure, Stations, Signals, LIRR, MNR business units.
MTA subsidiary mentioned in Personal Property Disposition Guidelines
Construction and development arm of the MTA responsible for capital planning and project delivery.
MTA Construction & Development Company
Provides oversight for the planning, design and construction of current and future major MTA system-wide expansion projects; formerly called MTA Capital Construction Company
Agency within MTA listed under February Financial Plan 2022 Adopted Budget positions
MTA Construction and Development Company (C&D)
The newly-created MTA Construction & Development unifying capital construction under one agency
MTA C&D responsible for delivering capital projects including LIRR, MNR, Stations, Infrastructure, Signals, Systems, and Bridges & Tunnels business units

Fifteen procurement actions brought for approval.

description: Sewer replacement at 207th Street Yard

Relocate NYC owned sewers from inside 207th Street Yard to outside. Remove and relocate existing DEP regulator, diversion chamber, tide gates. Install reinforced sewer lines and back-water valves. Budget $152.4M. NTP Dec 27, 2019. Substantial completion Feb 2024.

Relocates existing NYC DEP interceptors from inside the 207th Street Yard onto NYC streets by micro-tunneling operations, constructs new regulators to control flows, and seals/abandons yard manholes to protect the yard from future storm events.
Relocation of NYC-owned sewers that backed up into 207th Street Yard during Super Storm Sandy.

Sewer relocation at 207th Street Yard. Original SC Feb 2024, revised to Jan 2025 (11-month delay). Third party utility delays and DOS moratorium. EAC $170M - over budget. Budget modification being prepared.

records.raw_text: 207th Street Yard sustain extensive water damage during Super Storm Sandy from the brackish water from the Harlem River.

CBTC from north of Union Turnpike to 179 St Station on the Queens Blvd (Hillside Av) Line (F). Four interlockings modernized. Will expand interoperability from two to three CBTC suppliers (adding Mitsubishi).

Installer: EJ Electric ($440.5M). CBTC Supplier: Mitsubishi ($98M). DCS: Siemens (in negotiation). First CBTC project adding Mitsubishi to interoperability.

description: Fortify Staten Island's Clifton Shop and Yard infrastructure and equipment against heavy rainfall and/or storm surges. Construct a brand-new maintenance facility to replace existing obsolescent buildings. Budget $211.5M. Original forecast beneficial use Jul 2020, current Sept 2021. Project is 17 months behind schedule.

New 93,000 sf maintenance facility for Staten Island Railroad (SIRTOA). Super storm resilient, four tracks, includes Supply Logistics and SIRTOA's first wheel truing machine for profiling both rail-car and locomotive wheel sets.

Clifton Shop (SIR). 94% complete. Beneficial use achieved. SC moved from June to October 2022 (may be delayed additional 2 months). Budget $212M. Potential unforeseen hazardous materials during demolition of old shop as top risk.

description: New digital radio system providing voice and data communications for all NYCT and MTA Bus operators and field supervisors. Includes 34 base stations, outfitting approximately 6,000 buses and 250 non-revenue vehicles, new Bus Command Center with dispatch consoles.
Upgrading the Bus Radio System for the Department of Buses
Radio system upgrade for buses; over 75% complete with substantial completion forecast August 2024
Replacement of NYCT and MTA Bus analog bus radio system with a fully integrated digital bus radio system providing voice and data communications for all operators and field supervisors

Modern voice and digital communication system equipping nearly 6,000 buses, fully integrated with the MTA's Bus Command Center. Included new hardware and software for computerized dispatch and 35 base stations across NYC, parts of New Jersey, and Yonkers.

Implement a new digital bus radio system for NYCT and MTA Bus. Includes constructing 35 new radio base station sites, retrofitting entire fleet of approx. 6,000 buses and non-revenue vehicles, furnishing new radio control and dispatch system in new Bus Command Center. Budget $293.8M. Original substantial completion Dec 2020, current Dec 2021.
Completion goal for Bus Radio System for NYCT and MTA Bus in 2021

Replaces antiquated 30+ year old radio communications system for NYCT and MTA Bus with new digital radio system. Includes 35 base radio sites in five boroughs, Yonkers, and Kearny NJ; outfitting approx. 6,000 buses and 250 non-revenue vehicles; and the new Bus Command Center.

Bus Radio System replacement for NYCT and MTA Bus. 67% complete. EAC $330M exceeds budget by $36M. IEC forecasts EAC $350M. Significant delays due to slow contractor progress on base stations, pilot bus installations. Contractor shows May 2024 SC; C&D driving to December 2023 SC. 350 buses/month needed.

records.display_name: Throgs Neck Bridge Orthotropic Deck Replacement (TN49)

description: Reconstruct suspended span roadway replacing 1960's concrete grid deck with lightweight steel orthotropic deck, steel repairs, strengthening of stiffening and floor beam trusses, painting of main trusses and floor trusses, replacement of concrete deck at anchorages and towers, new dry fire standpipe from Bronx approach to Queens anchorage, new drainage system, overhead sign gantries, lighting improvements. Contract TN49 awarded November 2018 to Judlau Contracting Inc. with 48-month duration. Deck work in six stages using movable barrier. Bridge returned to full 6-lane configuration September 2022.

Staged replacement of existing concrete deck with new steel orthotropic deck to improve wind performance, structural resiliency, and meet seismic/fatigue criteria. Scope includes steel repairs, drainage improvements, suspender rope replacement, main cable inspection, and utility system improvements.

Replacement of the suspended span roadway deck at the Throgs Neck Bridge. Replaces original 1960's concrete grid deck with lightweight steel orthotropic deck, steel repairs, painting, lighting upgrades, and fire standpipe system.

Orthotropic deck replacement at Throgs Neck Bridge. 70% complete, within budget at $336M, on schedule for SC November 2022. EAC $310M. Early construction issues being mitigated by resequencing. Minor scope transfer of suspender rope and main cable work to future project.
project_name: Throgs Neck Bridge Orthotropic Deck Replacement
Throgs Neck Bridge Orthotropic Deck Replacement (TN-49)
status: Approximately 92% complete, Substantial Completion forecasted December 2022 (on target)

payload.description: Reconstruct suspended span roadway replacing 1960's concrete grid deck with lightweight steel orthotropic deck, steel repairs, strengthening of stiffening and floor beam trusses, painting of main trusses and floor trusses, replacement of concrete deck at anchorages and towers, new dry fire standpipe from Bronx approach to Queens anchorage, new drainage system, overhead sign gantries, lighting improvements. Contract TN49 awarded November 2018 to Judlau Contracting Inc. with 48-month duration. Deck work in six stages using movable barrier. Bridge returned to full 6-lane configuration September 2022.
payload.project_name: Throgs Neck Bridge Orthotropic Deck Replacement
payload.status: Approximately 92% complete, Substantial Completion forecasted December 2022 (on target)

records.raw_text: Project TN-49 is the staged replacement of the existing concrete deck with a new steel orthotropic deck. The orthotropic deck design will improve the bridge's wind performance, structural resiliency, and upgrade the suspended spans to meet current seismic and fatigue performance criteria. The contract was awarded November 2018 to Judlau Contracting Inc for $235M. The project's duration is 48 months and in addition to above deck replacement, the project's scope includes: Additional steel repairs to the supporting structure; Increasing deck cross slopes to improve drainage; Removal and replacement of four suspender ropes for testing; Main cable opening and inspection; Improving bridge utility systems.

description: Rehabilitation of the 207th Street Yard. Budget/EAC $643M.
Coney Island Yard comprehensive storm protection; 207th Street Yard strategic alignment of five projects under one project management team
Flood protection and sewer replacement at NYCT's 207th Street Yard, including flood walls, signal buildings, track replacement, and interceptor sewer

Major resiliency and infrastructure upgrade at the second largest transit facility in NYC after Coney Island Yard. Included 2,100 feet of flood-resilient walls, replacement of 35,000 feet of track and 83 switches, signal and power system upgrades, new signal and Rapid Transit Office tower, and new water line for fire suppression. Completed on schedule and $8 million under budget.

Repair damage to the 207 St Yard caused by Hurricane Sandy and fortify against future flooding. Six components: Perimeter Walls and Gates, Tunnel Portal, Traction Power Cable Replacement, Signals, Yard Track, Yard Switches. Budget $638M. Substantial completion Nov 2023.

Repairs Hurricane Sandy damage to the 43-acre 207th Street Yard. Work includes perimeter protection wall, two new signal relay buildings, track and interlocking repair, yard traction power replacement. Serves A and C subway lines, repair yard for ABCD lines, overhaul facility for 1-7 lines.
Flood mitigation at 207th Street Yard including perimeter flood wall, signal relay buildings, track and signal work, and traction power replacement.

Flood mitigation at 207th Street Yard and Shop. 72% complete. EAC $633M. SC forecast May 2024 (6-month delay from Nov 2023). Delay due to late procurement and delivery of signal equipment. Signal contractor's performance must improve. Flood wall, track improvements and facilities making good progress.

Modification for purchase and installation of additional signal wiring for the new signal Program Logic Controller (PLC) system being installed in the Coney Island Yard.
